title: Lowest Common Ancestor in BST
description:
Given a BST and two node values p and q that exist in the tree, return the value of their lowest common ancestor.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ nodes ≤ 10^5
- All values unique
- p and q are present
explanation:
Intuition
BST ordering guides the search. If both targets are less than current, move left; if both greater, move right; otherwise current is the split point.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Normalize p≤q
2. Main Algorithm: Walk down from root following BST directions
3. Return Result: Return first node where p≤node≤q

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(h)
- Space Complexity: O(1) iterative

Key Insights
- Split point property characterizes the LCA
- Iterative walk keeps space constant

Common Pitfalls
- Not normalizing order of p and q complicates compares
- Confusing equality handling at ancestor

testCases.explanation: One is ancestor of the other
